papers.title: MASEval: Extending Multi-Agent Evaluation from Models to Systems
papers.innovation: Proposes a crucial shift from model-centric to system-centric evaluation for LLM-based agentic systems. It identifies and emphasizes the need to benchmark system components beyond just the underlying LLM, including frameworks, protocols, tool usage, memory, and planning, which is vital for real-world deployments.
papers.benchmarks: Argues that existing benchmarks are insufficient for evaluating full agentic systems and implicitly calls for new methodologies to assess the performance and robustness of system-level components and their interactions.
papers.use_case: Guiding AI engineers in the informed selection, design, and optimization of various components (e.g., agentic frameworks, memory modules, planning algorithms, tool usage strategies) within complex multi-agent LLM systems, leading to more robust and performant deployments.

papers.title: Quantifying the Accuracy and Cost Impact of Design Decisions in Budget-Constrained Agentic LLM Search
papers.innovation: Provides an empirical study that quantifies the critical trade-offs between accuracy and cost based on key design decisions (e.g., search depth, retrieval strategy, completion token budget) within agentic Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems. This offers practical, data-driven insights for resource-constrained environments.
papers.benchmarks: Involves controlled measurement studies and empirical evaluation of how specific design parameters affect both accuracy and operational cost in agentic RAG systems, providing concrete data for decision-making.
papers.use_case: Optimizing agentic RAG systems for real-world deployment where explicit budgets on tool calls and completion tokens are common. It enables engineers to make informed, data-driven decisions on system configuration to balance performance, quality, and cost-efficiency.

papers.title: AgentOS: From Application Silos to a Natural Language-Driven Data Ecosystem
papers.innovation: Introduces the concept of an 'AgentOS' to manage and operate locally hosted intelligent agents, enabling them to autonomously control computing environments, orchestrate complex workflows, and integrate diverse data sources, thereby moving beyond fragmented application silos. It emphasizes control, safety, and a unified data ecosystem.
papers.benchmarks: Demonstrates the practical integration and orchestration capabilities with existing systems like OpenClaw, showcasing its potential to manage local computing resources and orchestrate complex, multi-step tasks across a system.
papers.use_case: Building the foundational infrastructure and operating environment for deploying and managing autonomous LLM-based agents within an enterprise. It empowers engineers to create systems where agents can interact seamlessly with local computing resources, diverse data, and orchestrate complex workflows, addressing current challenges of application isolation.
